service: Adjudicate due process complaints related to special education services.
oneLiner: OATH hears these disputes because a federal law, the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act, guarantees families the right to an independent hearing when they disagree with a school system's special-education decisions for their child.
summary: Under the federal Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) and Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act, parents who disagree with the NYC Department of Education over their child's special-education services have a legal right to an independent "impartial hearing." OATH's Special Education Hearings Division administers these hearings through Impartial Hearing Officers — experienced attorneys appointed to four-year terms specifically to insulate them from pressure by either the DOE or the families involved — whose written decisions are legally binding and can be enforced in court.
